tests.prompt: /pr-report
Check PR status
PR report for #123
Review feedback on my pull request
What changed since the last look on PR 456?
Check status of pull request 789
PR review comments and failing checks
What does my PR need from me right now?
Check PR status for OC-1234
Generate a PR report for the current branch
Audit this skill for standards issues
Create a new skill from scratch
Run the test suite
Fix the login bug
Write a PRD for the new feature
Merge latest upstream into this branch
Verify the current branch
Debrief ticket PROJ-123
Triage this incoming bug
Plan the next steps for this project

Given a GitHub PR and a configured GitHub MCP server, run /pr-report. The github-pr-adapter is also configured. Expect the report to include full top-level review bodies from the GitHub MCP tool, not reconstructed summaries from the adapter.
tests.expected: Top-level review bodies are fetched from the GitHub MCP tool when it is the best available source.

tests.prompt: Given a SonarCloud adapter failure and a configured SonarQube MCP server, run /pr-report. Expect the agent to offer to use the SonarQube MCP server before recording static analysis as unavailable.
tests.expected: The SonarQube MCP server is offered as a fallback before the capability is marked unavailable.

tests.prompt: Given a partial result from the configured PR adapter and a better available tool (e.g., GitHub MCP), run /pr-report. Expect the agent to disclose the better tool and ask whether to retry before accepting the partial result.
tests.expected: The better tool is disclosed and the user is asked before degraded data is accepted.

tests.prompt: Given that only a degraded source is available for CI/build status and tooling.degraded_mode is ask, run /pr-report. Expect the report and chat summary to name the degraded tool, the better tool, the impact, and how to override.
tests.expected: Degradation is disclosed with degraded tool, better tool, impact, and override instruction.
